The Emperor consulting an Oracle, the Demon gives no Response, being awed by the Nearness of Babylas the Martyr.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: The Emperor consulting an Oracle, the Demon gives no Response, being awed by the Nearness of Babylas the Martyr.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Having ordered that the pagan temples at Antioch should be opened, he was very eager to obtain an oracle from Apollo of Daphne.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But the demon that inhabited the temple remained silent through fear of his neighbor, Babylas the martyr; for the coffin which contained the body of that saint was close by.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: When the emperor was informed of this circumstance, he commanded that the coffin should be immediately removed: upon which the Christians of Antioch, including women and children, transported the coffin from Daphne to the city, with solemn rejoicings and chanting of psalms.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The psalms were such as cast reproach on the gods of the heathen, and those who put confidence in them and their images.
